phrases.text_local: Où commence la rue des Teinturiers ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo koh-MAHNS lah roo day tan-too-RYAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Rue des Teinturiers is one of Avignon's most picturesque streets, running along the Sorgue river.

responses.english: From Place des Corps-Saints, straight ahead.
responses.phonetic: duh-PWEE lah plahs day kor-SAN, too DRWAH
responses.local: Depuis la place des Corps-Saints, tout droit.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there are restaurants along the street
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il des restaurants dans cette rue ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL day res-to-RAHN dahn set ROO
phrases.cultural_note: The street has restaurants and cafes with terraces along the canal. Very popular in summer evenings.

responses.english: Yes, several with terraces along the canal.
responses.phonetic: wee, plu-ZYUHR ah-VEK teh-RAHS oh bor doo kah-NAL
responses.local: Oui, plusieurs avec terrasse au bord du canal.

phrases.text_local: Où sont les meilleurs cafés de la rue ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo son lay may-YUHR kah-FAY duh lah ROO
phrases.cultural_note: The street has excellent cafés with outdoor seating by the canal. Try 'Café de la Banque' or 'Le Mistral'.

responses.english: There are several along the street, with outdoor seating.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ahn ah plu-ZYUHR luh lohn duh lah ROO, ah-VEK teh-RAHS
responses.local: Il y en a plusieurs le long de la rue, avec terrasse.

phrases.cultural_note: The street has artisan shops, boutiques, and antique stores. It's one of Avignon's most charming areas.

responses.english: Artisan shops and antique stores, mostly.
responses.phonetic: day boo-TEEK dar-tee-ZAHN ay day zahn-tee-KEHR, sur-TOO
responses.local: Des boutiques d'artisans et des antiquaires, surtout.
